market_news.title: Stock Market Live January 12, 2026: S&P 500 (SPY) Sinks on DOJ Investigation into Powell
market_news.content: The stock market saw significant drops as the S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq declined due to a DOJ criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell concerning his Senate Banking Committee testimony on Fed office renovations. Investors sought safe havens, leading to a surge in gold and silver prices. Additionally, President Trump's call for a one-year 10% cap on credit card interest rates impacted credit card stocks, while Walmart gained after news of its inclusion in the NASDAQ 100.

Steve Dertien of PTC highlights how AI can free engineers from time-consuming change management tasks to focus on innovation, optimize supply chains, and accelerate time to market. items.content: This article highlights top stock reports from Zacks Research Daily, focusing on Microsoft Corp. (MSFT), Eli Lilly and Co. (LLY), and Lam Research Corp. (LRCX). It details their financial performance, market positions, key growth drivers, and challenges. The report also includes analysis of micro-cap Ampco-Pittsburgh Corp. (AP) and mentions other companies like Marsh & McLennan, Ross Stores, and Cardinal Health.

items.title: Apple will use Google’s Gemini models to power new Siri release
items.content: Apple Inc. has announced a multi-year partnership with Google LLC to integrate Google's Gemini series of large language models into its Siri voice assistant. The upgraded Siri, part of Apple's new AI suite called Apple Intelligence, is expected to provide more personalized responses and handle multi-step tasks across various apps, rolling out later this year. This collaboration has also reportedly pushed Alphabet Inc.'s market capitalization above $4 trillion, making it the fourth company to rea

items.title: Microsoft expands its presence at UC
items.content: Microsoft has significantly expanded its presence at the University of Cincinnati's 1819 Innovation Hub, more than doubling its space to nearly 3,700 square feet. This investment highlights Microsoft's commitment to Southwest Ohio and leveraging UC's student talent for AI training, workshops, and fostering innovation. The expansion aims to strengthen ties with Bearcat talent and local businesses, contributing to the region's entrepreneurial and innovation ecosystems.

items.title: Adobe Firefly gets GPT-Image 1.5 support, limited-time free images
items.content: Adobe Firefly now supports OpenAI’s GPT-Image 1.5 model, allowing Firefly Pro and Premium subscribers to generate unlimited images with this model until January 15. This integration is part of Adobe's broader strategy to incorporate AI-based features across its products, including Photoshop, Illustrator, and Adobe Express. Firefly offers various subscription plans based on generative credits, which are used for generating high-quality image, vector, video, and audio outputs.

items.title: Apple Teams Up With Google for A.I. in Its Products
items.content: Apple announced a partnership with Google to base its upcoming Apple Intelligence models on Google's Gemini A.I. models and cloud computing services. This move comes after Apple faced increasing scrutiny for its slower adoption of artificial intelligence compared to other tech giants. The collaboration will power Apple's Siri and other A.I. features, though the deal's financial terms were not disclosed, and the partnership is not exclusive.

items.title: Key facts: Goldman Sachs raises Microsoft target; Bank of America sees $300B bonds; Microsoft upgrades Foundry
items.content: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for Microsoft (MSFT) to $655, maintaining a Buy rating, citing AI sector strength. Bank of America projects that Microsoft and other major tech companies will issue between $140 billion and $300 billion in corporate bonds annually for the next three years to fund AI investments. Microsoft has also enhanced its Foundry platform with Claude integration, specifically benefiting healthcare and life sciences clients.

items.title: Equinix Inc.: The Quiet Super?Infrastructure Powering the Cloud Wars
items.content: Equinix Inc. has evolved beyond a traditional data center operator to become a critical software-defined backbone for the internet and multi-cloud environments. The company's strategy focuses on neutrality, interconnection services, and a global, consistent platform to cater to growing demands from AI, cloud, and data-intensive applications. This positioning allows Equinix to maintain a premium valuation as a growth-oriented digital infrastructure asset in a competitive market.

items.title: Apple calls on Google to help smarten up Siri and bring other AI features to the iPhone
items.content: Apple is partnering with Google to integrate Google's Gemini AI technology into its "Apple Intelligence" suite for the iPhone and other products. This collaboration aims to enhance Siri and other AI features, addressing Apple's previous delays in the AI race. The deal is seen as a significant validation for Google, whose parent company, Alphabet, recently surpassed Apple in market value, driven by its AI advancements.

items.title: ServiceNow stock steadies after Goldman’s fresh “Buy” call as earnings near
items.content: ServiceNow shares slightly increased after Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a "Buy" rating and a $205 price target, providing a lift following a two-day drop. Investors are now closely watching the company's Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings report on January 28, especially for signs that AI hype is translating into actual bookings and renewals. Despite a positive analyst call, concerns remain about federal business flatlining and potentially uneven AI adoption impacting near-term performance.

items.title: Gartner lista as empresas que são referências na corrida dos fornecedores de IA
items.content: Gartner identificou as "Companies to Beat" em cerca de 30 categorias de IA, baseando-se em critérios como recursos técnicos, implementações de clientes, modelo de negócios e parcerias. O relatório destaca Google pela IA agêntica, Palo Alto Networks para segurança de IA e Microsoft para IA corporativa. A competição no mercado de IA está se intensificando, com o Gartner prevendo que essas avaliações evoluirão conforme o setor amadurece.

items.title: Sizing Up Whether The Global Select Equity ETF Is A Buy Right Now
items.content: The JPMorgan Global Select Equity ETF (JGLO) launched in September 2023 with $7.1 billion in assets and a 0.47% expense ratio. The ETF, which has gained 15% over the past year, is heavily concentrated in six mega-cap tech stocks, holding over a quarter of its assets in NVIDIA, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, Alphabet, and Meta. items.title: Commonwealth Fusion Systems Appoints Stephane Bancel To Board
items.content: Commonwealth Fusion Systems (CFS), a leading private fusion company, announced that Stephane Bancel, Moderna Inc.'s CEO, has joined its Board of Directors as an Independent Member. Bancel, known for leading Moderna's COVID-19 vaccine development, brings expertise in scaling innovative technologies. He was also an early investor in CFS and aims to help commercialize fusion energy for global power demand.

items.title: Microsoft Stock Can Grow 37%, Goldman Sachs Says. It Has Many Paths to AI Profit.
items.content: Goldman Sachs believes Microsoft stock could surge by 37% due to its diversified investments in artificial intelligence. The report highlights Microsoft's historical success in benefiting from major technological shifts, positioning it strongly for the AI era.

items.title: Why Alphabet (GOOGL) stock is up today: Google shares jump over 1% as Alphabet becomes the fourth company
items.content: Alphabet (GOOGL) shares surged over 1% on January 12, 2026, pushing its market capitalization beyond $4 trillion for the first time, making it the fourth company to reach this milestone. This rally is primarily driven by a multi-year AI partnership with Apple, making Google's Gemini the AI backbone for Siri, along with strong Google Cloud growth and successful Gemini 3 model rollout. items.title: PayPal's Agentic Commerce Expansion: Will it Boost Top-Line Growth?
items.content: PayPal recently announced a strategic partnership with Microsoft to integrate Copilot Checkout into Copilot, enabling seamless shopping experiences. This is part of PayPal's broader push into "agentic commerce" through collaborations with various platforms like OpenAI, Perplexity, Wix, and Shopify, aiming to leverage AI for enhanced discovery and checkout processes. Despite these initiatives, PayPal's shares have declined 22% in the last six months, though the stock appears undervalued with a Va

items.title: PayPal's Agentic Commerce Expansion: Will it Boost Top-Line Growth?
items.content: PayPal is expanding its agentic commerce initiatives through strategic partnerships with Microsoft, OpenAI, and Perplexity, integrating its payment services into AI-driven platforms. These collaborations aim to enable seamless shopping experiences within AI interfaces, allowing merchants to convert product catalogs into purchasable items. Despite these efforts, PayPal's stock has underperformed, though its valuation appears attractive, with analysts expecting future EPS growth.

items.title: Microsoft Pulls The Plug On Lens, Shifts Mobile Scanning To OneDrive
items.content: Microsoft is discontinuing its standalone Lens app and integrating its mobile scanning capabilities into OneDrive. This move is part of a larger strategy to consolidate productivity tools under the Microsoft 365 Copilot umbrella. The Lens app, which had a loyal following, will be removed from app stores on February 9, 2026, with scanning functionality ceasing a month later.

items.title: Alphabet hits $4 trillion valuation as AI refocus lifts sentiment
items.content: Alphabet achieved a $4 trillion market valuation, driven by its intensified focus on artificial intelligence, following a deal for Apple's AI models to be based on Google's Gemini. The company's stock has surged about 65% in 2025, outperforming peers, and it has also seen significant growth in its Google Cloud unit and a stable advertising business. This milestone makes Alphabet the fourth company to reach this valuation, reflecting a significant shift in investor sentiment.

items.title: Microsoft plans to clarify confusing Windows 11 driver names
items.content: Microsoft is addressing criticism regarding the unclear naming of Windows 11 drivers, which often lack details about the device or purpose. The company plans to provide more descriptive metadata for drivers in future updates. However, logistical challenges with partners mean that clearer driver names may take some time to implement without a confirmed timeline.

items.title: Google-Parent Alphabet Becomes Newest $4 Trillion Company, Joining Nvidia
items.content: Alphabet's market capitalization briefly surpassed $4 trillion, making it the latest tech company to hit this valuation, driven by optimism over its AI advancements. This surge follows the successful launch of its Gemini 3 model and Apple's decision to integrate Gemini into Siri. Alphabet joins Nvidia above the $4 trillion mark, while Apple and Microsoft have fallen slightly below it.

items.title: Smart Data-Driven Pest Control
items.content: Rentokil Initial plc has developed a "Smart Data-Driven Pest Control" system utilizing IoT, sensor networks, and cloud-based analytics for real-time monitoring and predictive pest management. This approach aims to improve responsiveness and resource allocation by shifting from scheduled visits to condition-based service delivery, reducing chemical use and operational disruption. The system provides prioritized work orders for technicians and dashboards for compliance, informing site design and s

items.title: Alphabet hits $4-trillion valuation as AI refocus lifts sentiment
items.content: Alphabet briefly reached a $4 trillion market valuation, driven by its intensified focus on artificial intelligence. This surge, which saw its class-A shares hit a record high, reflects growing investor confidence and follows the company surpassing Apple in market capitalization for the first time since 2019. The company's AI efforts, including its Google Cloud unit and the Gemini models, have contributed significantly to its strong performance and market leadership.

items.title: 'Big Short' Investor Michael Burry Bets Against Oracle—Here's His Advice
items.content: Michael Burry, known for his "Big Short" bet against the 2008 housing market, has disclosed he owns put options on Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL) and has previously shorted the stock. Burry believes Oracle's significant debt from expanding into cloud computing services presents a strategic vulnerability. Despite his bearish stance on Oracle, he remains hesitant to short larger tech companies like Meta, Alphabet, and Microsoft, believing their diverse businesses will weather potential challenges.

items.title: Accelsius Closes $65 Million Series B Funding Led by Johnson Controls; Legrand Joining Round to Scale Liquid Cooling for Gigawatt-Class AI Factories – Company Announcement - FT.com
items.content: Accelsius, a leader in two-phase, direct-to-chip liquid cooling for AI and high-performance computing, has closed a $65 million Series B funding round. The round was led by Johnson Controls, with strategic investment from Legrand, to scale liquid cooling solutions for AI factories. This investment will accelerate Accelsius' expansion, global reach, and the deployment of its proprietary NeuCool® technology, crucial for managing the extreme thermal demands of next-generation AI and HPC chips.

This expansion aims to meet the demand for privacy-preserving, decentralized AI infrastructure, particularly for workloads that cannot run on Big Tech platforms due to privacy and data sovereignty concerns. items.title: AlphaTON Capital Closes $46M AI Infrastructure Expansion to
items.content: AlphaTON Capital has announced a $46 million AI infrastructure expansion to address the demand for confidential compute, involving the deployment of 576 NVIDIA B300 chips. The company aims to provide alternatives to Big Tech infrastructure for AI workloads requiring data sovereignty and privacy.

items.title: SocGen Turns to Microsoft’s Copilot After Scrapping Own AI Tool
items.content: Societe Generale SA is replacing its internally developed AI tool, SoGPT, with Microsoft Corp.'s Copilot solution. The French lender made this decision after employees found SoGPT to be outdated and its performance lagged behind other available tools. This move highlights the significant challenges and costs even large financial institutions face in developing and maintaining their own competitive AI technology.

items.title: ’Big Short’ investor Burry opens put position against Oracle By Investing.com
items.content: Michael Burry, known for "The Big Short," has opened a put option position against Oracle Corp. He criticized Oracle's recent strategy and also shorted the company directly. Burry has previously taken bearish stances on Nvidia and Palantir due to AI concentration.

items.title: AMETEK, Inc. $AME Shares Purchased by Tokio Marine Asset Management Co. Ltd.
items.content: Tokio Marine Asset Management Co. Ltd. increased its stake in AMETEK, Inc. by 9.9% to 59,049 shares, now valued at $11.10 million. This comes as AMETEK exceeded Q3 earnings and revenue estimates, with strong guidance for Q4 2025 and FY2025, leading several analysts to raise price targets. Institutional investors currently own 87.43% of the company's stock, despite recent insider share sales.

items.content: The stock market shows signs of broadening beyond U.S. large-cap growth stocks due to factors like increased capital expenditure by mega-caps, converging earnings growth across market segments, and favorable relative valuations. The authors suggest that the AI arms race is making asset-light U.S. mega-caps more capital intensive, potentially dampening their future returns while benefiting other market segments. items.title: Radware Uncovers ZombieAgent, a Zero-Click AI Vulnerability in OpenAI Agents
items.content: Radware has discovered "ZombieAgent," a zero-click indirect prompt injection vulnerability affecting OpenAI's Deep Research agent. This vulnerability allows for invisible data theft, persistent agent hijacking, and service-side execution that bypasses traditional security controls, operating entirely within OpenAI's cloud infrastructure. ZombieAgent can remain persistent by implanting malicious rules in an agent's long-term memory and can autonomously propagate across an organization.

items.content: TD Cowen has adjusted its price target for Fiserv (FISV) to $77 from $80 while maintaining a Hold rating. This news was published on January 12, 2026, at 07:10 AM EST by MT Newswires. Fiserv specializes in IT solutions for the financial and insurance sectors, with its primary revenue coming from online banking and investment support.

items.title: Data center investments to hit $3 trillion over next five years, says Moody’s
items.content: Moody's Ratings predicts that data center investments will reach $3 trillion over the next five years, driven by the growth of artificial intelligence and cloud computing. Major tech companies, including six US hyperscalers, are expected to lead this surge, investing specifically $500 billion in data centers this year. The increased capital requirements will involve more institutional investors alongside banks and a rise in asset-backed securities and other private credit markets for financing.
